title: Work With Variable Data In C# Console Applications - Quiz
description: Test your understanding of value vs reference types, string methods, arrays, array methods, string formatting, and composite formatting in C#

questions.question: In C#, which of the following is a value type?
questions.explanation: `int` is a value type — it stores the actual value directly on the stack. `string`, `object`, and arrays are reference types — they store a reference (pointer) to the data on the heap. Other value types include `bool`, `double`, `char`, and `struct`.

questions.question: What does the `string.Trim()` method do?

options.text: Removes all spaces from the string
Removes leading and trailing whitespace from the string
Shortens the string to a specified length
Removes the first and last characters

questions.explanation: `Trim()` removes whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines) from both the beginning and end of a string. Use `TrimStart()` for leading whitespace only or `TrimEnd()` for trailing whitespace only.

questions.question: What does `"Hello World".Contains("World")` return?
questions.explanation: `Contains()` returns a Boolean indicating whether the specified substring exists within the string. Since "Hello World" contains "World", it returns `true`. Note that `Contains()` is case-sensitive by default.

questions.question: What is the result of `"Hello World".Substring(6, 5)`?
questions.explanation: `Substring(startIndex, length)` extracts a substring starting at the given index for the specified length. Index 6 is 'W' (0-based: H=0, e=1, l=2, l=3, o=4, space=5, W=6), and length 5 gives "World".

questions.question: What does `"Hello".Replace("l", "r")` return?
questions.explanation: `Replace()` replaces ALL occurrences of the specified string. "Hello" has two 'l' characters, and both are replaced with 'r', giving "Herro". The method returns a new string; the original is unchanged.

questions.question: How do you sort an array in place in C#?
questions.explanation: `Array.Sort(myArray)` is the static method that sorts the array in place in ascending order. Arrays don't have an instance Sort() method. `OrderBy()` is a LINQ method that returns a new sequence without modifying the original.

questions.question: What is the key difference between value types and reference types in C#?

options.text: Value types can be null; reference types cannot
Value types store the actual data; reference types store a reference (pointer) to the data
Value types are slower than reference types
Reference types can only store numbers

questions.explanation: Value types (int, bool, struct, etc.) store their data directly in the variable. Reference types (string, array, class instances) store a reference (memory address) pointing to the actual data on the heap. When you copy a value type, you get a separate copy; when you copy a reference type, both variables point to the same object.

questions.question: What is the output of `string.Format("Name: {0}, Age: {1}", "Alice", 30)`?
questions.explanation: `String.Format()` replaces numbered placeholders ({0}, {1}, etc.) with the corresponding arguments. {0} is replaced by "Alice" and {1} by 30, producing "Name: Alice, Age: 30". This is called composite formatting.

questions.question: What method reverses the order of elements in an array?
questions.explanation: `Array.Reverse(myArray)` is the static method that reverses the elements of the array in place. Like `Array.Sort()`, it modifies the original array.

questions.question: Strings in C# are immutable. What does this mean?

options.text: Strings cannot be assigned to variables
Once created, a string's value cannot be changed — any modification creates a new string object
Strings can only contain letters, not numbers

questions.explanation: String immutability means the actual string data in memory cannot be modified after creation. Methods like `Replace()`, `ToUpper()`, and `Trim()` all return NEW string objects — the original string remains unchanged. For frequent string modifications, use `StringBuilder` for better performance.
